mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in CPIAUCSL indicates disinflation, reducing central bank intervention pressure and boosting equity valuations through lower real interest rates, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in disinflation periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in UNRATE above 5.5% signals a deteriorating labor market, reducing corporate earnings visibility and causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in industrial production (INDPRO) and GDP (GDPC1) signals robust supply-side expansion, driving corporate profits and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC drops below 0.05 for any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day returns in co-expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening cycle, rising FEDFUNDS increases discount rates, reducing equity valuations and causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in housing starts (HOUST) and real sales (RSAFS) signal strong consumer demand, boosting corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC drops below 0.05 for any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day returns in co-demand expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) indicates a robust labor market, increasing consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite positive signal.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in CPIAUCSL signals rising inflation, prompting central bank rate hikes that depress equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained quarterly growth in GDP (GDPC1) indicates strong economic expansion, driving corporate profits and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC drops below 0.03 for any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
